SAFE WORKING PRACTICE
SWP-027
Method Statement

Tilt Table Testing Procedure

Issue Date
—
Next Review Date
—
Completed By
—
Approved By
—
Reviewed By
—
🔧 Plant, Tools & Equipment
Tilt Table
Test weights (combination)
Chains, shackles & ratchet straps
Spacer blocks & wheel chocks
Steel beam
Protective coverings for paintwork
PPE
👤 Personnel & Competencies
Trained and authorised personnel ONLY
Tilt table operator training
H&S Awareness / PPE Training
⚠ Hazard Analysis
Unstable/tipping load
Crushing/pinch points
Falling test weights
Unplanned movement of truck
Struck by equipment
📝 Sequence of Work
Tilt Testing is a hazardous operation and must be only undertaken by trained and authorised personnel. This SWP must be followed. If you have any doubt about the safety of the actions, do NOT proceed and report to line manager immediately.
Preparation
1
Ensure you have the appropriate chains, shackles and any additional equipment required for the task- carryout pre use checks.
2
Identify test weights required - carryout pre use checks.
3
Tilt Table - carryout pre use checks.
4
Locate spacer blocks & wheel chocks
5
Familiarise yourself with the relevant stability test to be undertaken or requested.
6
Ensure truck to be tested is at the required specification.
7
Erect barriers to exclude unauthorised personnel from tilt table and surrounding area (approx. 20m around tilt table)
8
Ascertain lift heights required for test & mark fixed stage of mast accordingly
9
Testing should ONLY be carried out by trained and authorised personnel
10
Lower table to horizontal position
11
Locate truck centrally on tilt table in correct orientation for test
12
Apply hand brake and then put truck direction lever into Neutral.
13
Attach restraining chains to chassis and mast to prevent total tip-over & adjust length. N.B. A small degree of slack chain is required to allow the truck to tilt before ultimate tip­ over. N.B. aim for approx.20mm off the tilt bed table to underside of tyre.
14
Restraining chains and straps should be attached in different directions to prevent the chassis from twisting at tip-over (see photos for guidance)
15
Chock wheels using blocks and steel beam to prevent the chassis from moving when tilted.
16
To minimize damage to truck paintwork, Use suitable coverings on chains.
Test Procedure
17
Place the required test weights onto forks - this may require a combination of test weights
18
Ensure - Load is applied centrally to fork carriage
19
Correct load centre is maintained - use spacer blocks
20
Combination of weights is close to required load. It is preferable for the load to be Slightly greater than required rather than less
21
Securely attach load (all weights) to forks/ fork carriage using ratchet straps to remove any possibility of the load separating from the truck
22
Lift load Approx. 500mm and obtain mast upright position using the tilt function.
23
With table horizontal, slowly lift load to required height.
24
Adjust mast tilt to give required position for the test to be conducted.
25
Fully tilted back position (for Reverse position tests).
26
Slowly continue to raise the table until tip-over occurs
27
Note the greatest amount of tilt at which the truck is still stable l.E. immediately before tip­ over occurs
28
Outer tyre lifting or chassis grounding IS permissible.
29
After tip-over, stabilise the truck by lowering the load, NOT by lowering the table as this can cause uncontrolled movement.
30
